title: NonVolatile::read returns Option<usize> while write returns Result - asymmetric error handling
description:
lichen-hal/src/lib.rs NonVolatile trait:
  pub trait NonVolatile {
      type Error;
      fn read(&self, key: &str, buf: &mut [u8]) -> Option<usize>;  // Option
      fn write(&mut self, key: &str, data: &[u8]) -> Result<(), Self::Error>;  // Result
      fn delete(&mut self, key: &str) -> bool;  // bool
  }

read() returns None for both 'key not found' and 'read error', losing error information.
delete() returns bool without error reporting.
Only write() has proper error propagation.

What goes wrong: Callers cannot distinguish between 'key does not exist' and 'storage failure' for reads. Error handling patterns differ between read and write operations on the same trait.
close_reason: Deliberate design - Option for read distinguishes 'not found' (None) from success (Some). Error enum would complicate simple lookups. Pattern matches Rust HashMap::get.
